commit_scope: Lift promoted BML read routes from router-only proof to handler and Python-authority proof in the source catalog.

observations: The promoted BML read routes were proven native by X-Form-Router after PR #2781, but their successful responses did not carry X-Form-Handler or X-Form-Python-Authority.
The BML catalog now has a narrow api-native-ok-json(handler, node) helper for successful native JSON responses that need semantic proof headers.
GET /api/runtime/events, /api/views/stats/{asset_id}, /api/reactions/concept/{entity_id}/summary, /api/reactions/concept/{entity_id}/threads, and /api/concepts/{concept_id}/voices now use the helper.
The Hostinger local canary is strict again for those promoted reads, requiring router, handler, and Python-authority proof headers from the newly built BML front-door image.

phase_gate.reason: Local source-catalog and canary-path proof passed; CI, merge, deploy, and public post-deploy header verification remain.

evidence_refs: post-merge public verifier for PR #2781 showed promoted BML reads router-native but handler=<missing>

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Promoted BML read routes emit X-Form-Handler and X-Form-Python-Authority: false on successful native JSON responses after the new BML front-door image is deployed.
test_flows: Build/load the BML source catalog through the Rust kernel route loader.
Run the Hostinger local canary and require router, handler, and Python-authority proof headers from the promoted read routes.
After deployment, curl the public promoted endpoints and verify X-Form-Handler and X-Form-Python-Authority are present.
